# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

export const config = {
  runtime: "edge",
};

const MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB max content size
const MAX_HEADER_VALUE_LENGTH = 8192; // 8KB max header value
const MAX_CUSTOM_HEADERS = 20;

const ALLOWED_CONTENT_TYPES = new Set([
  "text/plain",
  "text/html",
  "text/csv",
  "application/json",
  "application/xml",
  "text/xml",
  "application/octet-stream",
]);

const FORBIDDEN_HEADERS = new Set([
  "host",
  "connection",
  "keep-alive",
  "transfer-encoding",
  "te",
  "trailer",
  "upgrade",
  "proxy-authorization",
  "proxy-authenticate",
  "set-cookie",
  "cookie",
  "authorization",
  "www-authenticate",
  "access-control-allow-origin",
  "access-control-allow-credentials",
  "access-control-allow-methods",
  "access-control-allow-headers",
  "access-control-expose-headers",
  "access-control-max-age",
  "origin",
  "referer",
  "server",
  "x-powered-by",
]);

function sanitizeHeaderValue(value: string): string {
  // Remove any characters that could enable header injection (CR, LF, null bytes)
  return value.replace(/[\r\n\0]/g, "").trim();
}

function isValidHeaderName(name: string): boolean {
  // RFC 7230: token = 1*tchar
  // tchar = "!" / "#" / "$" / "%" / "&" / "'" / "*" / "+" / "-" / "." /
  //          "^" / "_" / "`" / "|" / "~" / DIGIT / ALPHA
  return /^[a-zA-Z0-9!#$%&'*+\-.^_`|~]+$/.test(name);
}

function validateAndSanitizeHeaders(
  userHeaders: unknown
): Record<string, string> | null {
  if (userHeaders === null || userHeaders === undefined) {
    return {};
  }

  if (typeof userHeaders !== "object" || Array.isArray(userHeaders)) {
    return null;
  }

  const sanitized: Record<string, string> = {};
  const entries = Object.entries(userHeaders as Record<string, unknown>);

  if (entries.length > MAX_CUSTOM_HEADERS) {
    return null;
  }

  for (const [key, value] of entries) {
    const lowerKey = key.toLowerCase();

    if (FORBIDDEN_HEADERS.has(lowerKey)) {
      continue; // Silently skip forbidden headers
    }

    if (!isValidHeaderName(key)) {
      return null;
    }

    if (typeof value !== "string") {
      return null;
    }

    if (value.length > MAX_HEADER_VALUE_LENGTH) {
      return null;
    }

    const sanitizedValue = sanitizeHeaderValue(value);
    sanitized[lowerKey] = sanitizedValue;
  }

  return sanitized;
}

function validateContentType(headers: Record<string, string>): boolean {
  const contentType = headers["content-type"];
  if (!contentType) {
    return true; // Will use default
  }

  // Extract the media type (before any parameters like charset)
  const mediaType = contentType.split(";")[0].trim().toLowerCase();
  return ALLOWED_CONTENT_TYPES.has(mediaType);
}

export default async function handler(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  if (request.method !== "POST") {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: "Method not allowed. Use POST." }),
      {
        status: 405,
        headers: {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          Allow: "POST",
        },
      }
    );
  }

  const requestContentType = request.headers.get("content-type") || "";
  if (!requestContentType.includes("application/json")) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: "Content-Type must be application/json" }),
      {
        status: 415,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  }

  const contentLength = request.headers.get("content-length");
  if (contentLength && parseInt(contentLength, 10) > MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        error: `Request body too large. Maximum size is ${MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH} bytes.`,
      }),
      {
        status: 413,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  }

  let body: unknown;
  try {
    const rawBody = await request.text();
    if (rawBody.length > MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH) {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({
          error: `Request body too large. Maximum size is ${MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH} bytes.`,
        }),
        {
          status: 413,
          headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        }
      );
    }
    body = JSON.parse(rawBody);
  } catch {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: "Invalid JSON in request body" }),
      {
        status: 400,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  }

  if (typeof body !== "object" || body === null || Array.isArray(body)) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: "Request body must be a JSON object" }),
      {
        status: 400,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  }

  const { content, headers: userHeaders } = body as {
    content?: unknown;
    headers?: unknown;
  };

  if (content === undefined || content === null) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: "Missing required field: content" }),
      {
        status: 400,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  }

  // Validate content - must be a string
  if (typeof content !== "string") {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: "Field 'content' must be a string" }),
      {
        status: 400,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  }

  if (content.length > MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        error: `Content too large. Maximum size is ${MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH} bytes.`,
      }),
      {
        status: 413,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  }

  // Validate and sanitize headers
  const sanitizedHeaders = validateAndSanitizeHeaders(userHeaders);
  if (sanitizedHeaders === null) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        error: `Invalid headers. Headers must be an object with string values, valid header names, values under ${MAX_HEADER_VALUE_LENGTH} bytes, and no more than ${MAX_CUSTOM_HEADERS} custom headers.`,
      }),
      {
        status: 400,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  }

  if (!validateContentType(sanitizedHeaders)) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        error: `Disallowed Content-Type. Allowed types: ${Array.from(ALLOWED_CONTENT_TYPES).join(", ")}`,
      }),
      {
        status: 400,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  }

  // Encode content to bytes for streaming
  const encoder = new TextEncoder();
  const contentBytes = encoder.encode(content);

  // Stream the content in chunks
  const CHUNK_SIZE = 1024; // 1KB chunks for smooth streaming

  const stream = new ReadableStream({
    start(controller) {
      let offset = 0;

      function pushChunk() {
        if (offset >= contentBytes.length) {
          controller.close();
          return;
        }

        const end = Math.min(offset + CHUNK_SIZE, contentBytes.length);
        const chunk = contentBytes.slice(offset, end);
        controller.enqueue(chunk);
        offset = end;

        // Use a small delay to simulate real streaming behavior
        // In production, this could be replaced with actual data source reads
        if (offset < contentBytes.length) {
          // Schedule next chunk
          setTimeout(pushChunk, 0);
        } else {
          controller.close();
        }
      }

      pushChunk();
    },
    cancel() {
      // Client disconnected, cleanup is handled automatically
    },
  });

  // Build response headers with security defaults
  const responseHeaders: Record<string, string> = {
    "Content-Type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8",
    "Cache-Control": "no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate",
    "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
    "X-Frame-Options": "DENY",
    "Content-Security-Policy": "default-src 'none'",
    "Referrer-Policy": "no-referrer",
  };

  // Apply user-provided headers (already validated and sanitized)
  // User headers can override Content-Type but not security headers
  const securityHeaders = new Set([
    "x-content-type-options",
    "x-frame-options",
    "content-security-policy",
    "referrer-policy",
    "strict-transport-security",
  ]);

  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(sanitizedHeaders)) {
    if (!securityHeaders.has(key)) {
      responseHeaders[key] = value;
    }
  }

  return new Response(stream, {
    status: 200,
    headers: responseHeaders,
  });
}
